pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot - Episode 2

pageCaption.value: Since 2016, with the help of local communities, Marine Conservation Cambodia has installed more than 300 artificial reefs in Mission Blue’s Kep Archipelago Hope Spot.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - The Kep Archipelago Hope Spot - Episode 2

metadescription.value: Conservationists in Cambodia are working to restore the Kep Archipelago and improve the livelihoods of those who rely on it. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Mission Blue: Kep Archipelago Hope Spot - Episode 2

title.value: Mission Blue: Kep Archipelago Hope Spot

text.value: Since 2016, with the help of local communities, Marine Conservation Cambodia has installed more than 300 artificial reefs in Mission Blue’s Kep Archipelago Hope Spot. The result has been the regrowth of some 1,000 hectares of seagrass, and increased sightings of endangered wildlife. Thanks to the overwhelming success of the project, the organization has been contracted by the Cambodian government to deploy artificial reefs along the country’s coastline. It will install 5,000 artificial reefs over the next five-and-a-half years.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

pageCaption.value: Mission Blue Hope Spot Champions Rili Djohani and Wira Sanjaya are working closely with communities in Bali to protect some of the world’s most biodiverse coral reefs.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Nusa Penida Hope Spot - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Founder of the Coral Triangle Center, Rili Djohani has dedicated her life to protecting Nusa Penida's coral reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Mount Logan Expedition Episode 1 - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Deep within the ice on the summit of Mount Logan, Canada’s highest peak, are clues about the Earth’s climate and atmosphere spanning thousands of years. Scientist and high-altitude mountaineer Alison Criscitiello led the Rolex and National Geographic Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition to retrieve these layers of past climates. What they find will help us understand past changing climates, and what they mean for our future.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex and National Geographic: Perpetual Planet Mount Logan Expedition - Episode 1

metadescription.value: Mountaineer Alison Criscitiello is expanding ice core research to new latitudes through her expedition to Mount Logan. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

pageCaption.value: With support from Rolex and the National Geographic Society, climate scientists and Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews endured snowstorms and sub-zero temperatures to install a state-of-the-art weather station on the summit of one of Peru’s highest mountains.

WaPageTitle.value: Perpetual Planet Amazon Expedition - Andes Mountains - Video

metadescription.value: Explorers Baker Perry and Tom Matthews have successfully installed a weather station in the high mountains of the Andes. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: Founded in 2008, Marine Conservation Cambodia has been installing artificial reefs to restore the depleted seabed around the Kep Archipelago since 2016.

metadescription.value: Conservationists are reviving Cambodia's Kep Archipelago by restoring seagrass meadows and deploying artificial reefs. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageCaption.value: The waters around Malpelo Island, situated hundreds of kilometres from the coast of Colombia, are home to breathtaking biodiversity.

pageTitle.value: Rolex and Mission Blue - Perpetual Planet

pageCaption.value: Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative supports those using science to understand the world’s environmental challenges and who are finding ways to restore balance to our ecosystems, particularly the oceans, which are under siege from a host of pressures.

metadescription.value: Through its Perpetual Planet initiative, Rolex supports Mission Blue in protecting marine ecosystems across the world. Watch the video on rolex.org.
